DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANT ARNOLD VALLERGA'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS - WHERE PLAINTIFF HAS RESPONDED THAT HE IS "NOT IN POSSESSION OF" DOCUMENTS TO THE EXTENT HE IS REFERRING TO ANYTHING OTHER THAN THE SEALED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, HE MUST SUPPLEMENT HIS RESPONSES OR PROVIDE A DECLARATION STATING EITHER THAT THERE ARE NO SUCH DOCUMENTS OR THAT HE EXERCISES REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN OBTAINING SUCH DOCUMENTS, IN WHICH CASE HE MUST IDENTIFY THEM. THE COURT AWARDED NO SANCTIONS. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED.

SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CAN NOT PLEAD FAVORABLE TERMINATION IN LIGHT OF THE DISMISSAL FOR A WAIVER OF COSTS PURSUANT TO A SETTLEMENT. ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL VALLERGA, EILEEN MCMULLEN-VALLERGA AND ARNOLD VALLERGA'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff can not plead favorable termination in light of the dismissal for a waiver of costs pursuant to a settlement.
The request by defendants Hart and Fell to be included on the order is Denied.
Plaintiff's counsel can not consent to the dismissal without the approval of his client.
Moving party to prepare a form of order.
Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8068 (301/PJB)
